cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a World Bank study from 1974 on economic growth in developing countries. The user wants to know what the study concluded regarding the benefits of economic growth. First, I need to refer back to the provided passage. The key part is in the third paragraph where it says: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. The study concluded that rapid growth had been of little benefit to a third of their population. That means that a third of the population didn't benefit much, while the remaining two-thirds might have. But the question is phrased as "brought ___". The options are whether the growth brought benefit to some group, or not. So the study found that despite the growth, only a third of the population (so most people did not benefit). The key is that the growth did not lead to development for most. The question is specifically about "economic growth in some backward countries brought _." The study's conclusion is that the growth was of little benefit to a third of their population. Therefore, the correct answer would be the one that says the growth brought little benefit to their people. Looking at the options: (A) benefit to a third of their population — the study says it was of little benefit to a third. So the benefit is not to a third, but that a third got little. So this might not be correct. (B) benefit to two thirds of their population — the study doesn't mention this. It's possible that two-thirds had some benefit, but the study's focus is that a third had little. But the passage says "little or no benefit coming to most of its citizens (development)" in the first paragraph. The study supports that. (C) little benefit to their people — this is a bit vague. The study says a third had little benefit. But "their people" as a whole would mean that for the majority, there was no benefit. But the option says "little benefit to their people," which might not be accurate because it was only a third. Wait, maybe I'm misinterpreting. The study says "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the majority (two-thirds) might have had some benefit. But the question is about what the study showed. The study's conclusion is that the growth was of little benefit to a third of their population. So the answer isn't (C) because it's only a third, not the entire population. But the options are (A), (B), (C), (D). Let me recheck the passage. The first paragraph says, "One can easily imagine instances in which a country has achieved higher levels of income (growth) with little or no benefit coming to most of its citizens (development)."
